title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk, but it could be leveraged by malicious actors to identify exploitable vulnerabilities in outdated or unpatched systems if misused. Additionally, there is a potential risk if the tool contains unverified or insecure code.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and ensure it adheres to secure coding practices before deploying it in production environments.
Educate users on the proper and ethical use of vulnerability scanning tools to prevent misuse.
Monitor the tool's updates and community feedback to stay informed about any emerging security concerns.

title: The WP-Optimize – Cache, Compress images, Minify & Clean database to boost page speed &...
description: The WP-Optimize – Cache, Compress images, Minify & Clean database to boost page speed & performance plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file deletion due to insufficient file path validation in the unscheduled_original_file_deletion function in all versions up to, and including, 4.5.2 This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with author-level access and above, to delete arbitrary files on the server, which can easily lead to remote code execution when the right file is del

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the WP-Optimize plugin allows authenticated attackers with author-level access or higher to delete arbitrary files on the server. This can lead to critical issues such as remote code execution, website defacement, or complete compromise of the affected WordPress site.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the WP-Optimize plugin to the latest patched version (if available) or disable it until a fix is released.
Restrict user roles and permissions to minimize the number of accounts with author-level access or higher.
Monitor server logs for suspicious activity, such as unauthorized file deletions, and implement file integrity monitoring.

title: An authenticated user can crash mongod when running $rankFusion or $scoreFusion with an empty...
description:
An authenticated user can crash mongod when running $rankFusion or $scoreFusion with an empty pipeline on a view.

When resolving a view, the server inspects the aggregation pipeline to determine whether it begins with an Atlas Search stage. For $rankFusion and $scoreFusion, this inspection reads the first element on each stage’s input pipeline array without first verifying that the array is non-empty. Supplying an empty pipeline causes a null pointer dereference and crashes the server.

This is

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8063.

title: The Slider Revolution plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Arbitrary File Upload in versions 7.0...
description: The Slider Revolution plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Arbitrary File Upload in versions 7.0.0 to 7.0.10 via the '_get_media_url' and '_check_file_path' function. This is due to insufficient file type validation. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with subscriber-level access and above, to upload files that may be executable, which makes remote code execution possible. The vulnerability was partially patched in version 7.0.10 and fully patched in version 7.0.11.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6692.

title: The BetterDocs Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the ...
description: The BetterDocs Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the `get_current_letter_docs` and `docs_sort_by_letter` AJAX actions in all versions up to, and including, 3.7.0. This is due to the `limit` POST parameter being interpolated directly into a SQL query string before being passed to `$wpdb->prepare()`, which only parameterizes other variables. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries that can be used

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SQL Injection vulnerability in the BetterDocs Pro plugin for WordPress allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL queries. This can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive database information, data exfiltration, data corruption, or even full compromise of the WordPress site.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the BetterDocs Pro plugin to a patched version once it becomes available. If no patch is available, temporarily disable the plugin to mitigate the risk.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious SQL injection attempts targeting the vulnerable endpoints.
Regularly review and monitor web server logs for unusual activity, such as unexpected SQL queries or unauthorized access attempts.

title: The Forminator Forms plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in versions up...
description: The Forminator Forms plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in versions up to and including 1.53.0. This is due to the listen_for_saving_export_schedule() function in library/class-export.php failing to perform a capability check before saving the scheduled export configuration, unlike the parallel listen_for_csv_export() function which correctly verifies user permissions. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers with subscriber-level access to configure a schedule

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with minimal access (subscriber-level) to configure scheduled exports, potentially leading to unauthorized data exposure or manipulation of export schedules. This could compromise sensitive information depending on the plugin's usage.
recommended_actions: Update the Forminator Forms plugin to the latest version as soon as a patch is released.
Restrict subscriber-level access and review user roles to minimize exposure.
Monitor scheduled export configurations for any unauthorized changes.

description:

Spring Cloud Config 3.1.x: affected from 3.1.0 through 3.1.13 (inclusive); upgrade to 3.1.14 or greater (Enterprise Support Only). Spring Cloud Config 4.1.x: affected from 4.1.0 through 4.1.9 (inclusive); upgrade to 4.1.10 or greater (Enterprise Support Only). Spring Cloud Config 4.2.x: affected from 4.2.0 through 4.2.6 (inclusive); upgrade to 4.2.7 or greater (Enterprise Support

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41004.

title: Spring Cloud Config allows applications to serve arbitrary text and binary files through the...
description:
Spring Cloud Config allows applications to serve arbitrary text and binary files through the spring-cloud-config-server module. A malicious user, or attacker, can send a request using a specially crafted URL that can lead to a directory traversal attack.
Spring Cloud Config 3.1.x: affected from 3.1.0 through 3.1.13 (inclusive); upgrade to 3.1.14 or greater (Enterprise Support Only). Spring Cloud Config 4.1.x: affected from 4.1.0 through 4.1.9 (inclusive); upgrade to 4.1.10 or greater (Enterprise

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform directory traversal attacks, potentially exposing sensitive files and data stored on the server. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, and compromise of critical systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade Spring Cloud Config to version 3.1.14 or greater for 3.1.x, or version 4.1.10 or greater for 4.1.x.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization to prevent exploitation of directory traversal vulnerabilities.
Monitor server logs for unusual activity or requests targeting the vulnerable module, and apply intrusion detection mechanisms.

title: When using Google Secrets Manager as a backend for the Spring Cloud Config server a client can...
description:
When using Google Secrets Manager as a backend for the Spring Cloud Config server a client can craft a request to the config server potentially exposing secrets from unintended GCP projects.
Spring Cloud Config 3.1.x: affected from 3.1.0 through 3.1.13 (inclusive); upgrade to 3.1.14 or greater (Enterprise Support Only). Spring Cloud Config 4.1.x: affected from 4.1.0 through 4.1.9 (inclusive); upgrade to 4.1.10 or greater (Enterprise Support Only). Spring Cloud Config 4.2.x: affected from 4.2.0 t

title: The base directory (`spring.cloud.config.server.git.basedir`) used by the Spring Cloud Config...
description:
The base directory (`spring.cloud.config.server.git.basedir`) used by the Spring Cloud Config Server to clone Git repositories to is susceptible to time-of-check-time-of-use (TOCTOU) attacks.
Spring Cloud Config 3.1.x: affected from 3.1.0 through 3.1.13 (inclusive); upgrade to 3.1.14 or greater (Enterprise Support Only). Spring Cloud Config 4.1.x: affected from 4.1.0 through 4.1.9 (inclusive); upgrade to 4.1.10 or greater (Enterprise Support Only). Spring Cloud Config 4.2.x: affected from 4.2.0

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Spring Cloud Config Server's handling of the base directory (`spring.cloud.config.server.git.basedir`) can allow attackers to exploit time-of-check-time-of-use (TOCTOU) weaknesses, potentially leading to unauthorized access or manipulation of sensitive configuration files. This could compromise application integrity and data security.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Spring Cloud Config to the latest patched versions: 3.1.14 or greater for 3.1.x, 4.1.10 or greater for 4.1.x, and ensure updates for 4.2.x as they become available.
Implement strict access controls and monitoring for the base directory to detect and prevent unauthorized modifications.
Conduct a thorough security audit of systems using Spring Cloud Config to identify potential exploitation of this vulnerability.

title: Diffusers has a `trust_remote_code` bypass via `custom_pipeline` and local custom components
description:
### Impact

A `trust_remote_code` bypass in `DiffusionPipeline.from_pretrained` allows arbitrary remote code execution despite the user passing `trust_remote_code=False` (or omitting it, which is the default). The vulnerability has three variants, all sharing the same root cause — the `trust_remote_code` gate was implemented inside `DiffusionPipeline.download()` rather than at the actual dynamic-module load site, so any code path that bypassed or short-circuited `download()` also bypassed the se

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows arbitrary remote code execution, enabling attackers to execute malicious code on affected systems despite security settings intended to prevent such actions. This can lead to data breaches, system compromise, or unauthorized access.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest patched version of the Diffusers library that addresses CVE-2026-44513.
Audit systems using the Diffusers library to identify any potential exploitation of this vulnerability and mitigate risks.
Implement strict network controls to limit the ability of remote code execution and monitor for suspicious activity.

title: Netty MQTT: Resource exhaustion in MqttDecoder
description:
### Impact
The MQTT 5 header Properties section is parsed and buffered _before_ any message size limit is applied.

Specifically, in `MqttDecoder`, the `decodeVariableHeader()` method is called before the `bytesRemainingBeforeVariableHeader > maxBytesInMessage` check. The `decodeVariableHeader()` can call other methods which will call `decodeProperties()`. Effectively, Netty does not apply any limits to the size of the properties being decoded.

Additionally, because `MqttDecoder` extends `Repla

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Netty's MQTT decoder allows attackers to exploit the lack of size limits on the properties section, potentially leading to resource exhaustion (e.g., memory overflows or denial of service). This could disrupt systems relying on MQTT for communication, particularly in environments with high message throughput.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of Netty that addresses CVE-2026-44248 and enforces size limits on MQTT properties.
Implement application-level message size validation to mitigate the risk of resource exhaustion until a patch is applied.
Monitor MQTT traffic for unusual patterns or spikes that could indicate exploitation attempts.

title: vm2 NodeVM `nesting: true` bypasses `require: false` allowing sandbox escape and arbitrary OS command execution
description:
### Summary

When a `NodeVM` is created with `nesting: true`, sandbox code can unconditionally `require('vm2')` regardless of the outer VM's `require` configuration — including `require: false`. With access to `vm2`, the sandbox constructs a new inner `NodeVM` with its own unrestricted `require` settings and executes arbitrary OS commands on the host. Any application that runs untrusted code inside a `NodeVM` with `nesting: true` is fully compromised.

### Details

The vulnerability is in how th

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44007.

title: vm2 has a NodeVM require.root bypass via symlink traversal that allows sandbox escape
description:
## Summary
NodeVM's `require.root` path restriction can be bypassed using filesystem symlinks, allowing sandboxed code to load modules from outside the allowed root directory in host context. Because path validation uses `path.resolve()` (which does not dereference symlinks) but module loading uses Node's native `require()` (which does), an attacker can load arbitrary host-realm modules and achieve remote code execution.

## Severity
**High** (CVSS 3.1: 8.5)

`CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-43998.

title: vm2's Transformer Fast-Path Bypass Exposes Internal State Variable
description:
### Summary
vm2's code transformer has a performance optimization that skips AST analysis when the code does not contain `catch`, `import`, or `async` keywords. This fast-path bypass allows sandboxed code to directly access the internal `VM2_INTERNAL_STATE_DO_NOT_USE_OR_PROGRAM_WILL_FAIL` variable, which exposes internal security functions (`handleException`, `wrapWith`, `import`).

### Details
In `lib/transformer.js:55-57`, a regex check `/\b(?:catch|import|async)\b/` determines whether AST tra

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44003.

title: vm2 is Vulnerable to Host File Path Disclosure via Stack Trace Information Leak
description:
### Summary
vm2's `CallSite` wrapper class (intended as a safe wrapper for V8's native CallSite) blocks `getThis()` and `getFunction()` to prevent host object leakage, but allows `getFileName()` to return unsanitized host absolute paths. Any sandboxed code can extract the full directory structure, library paths, and framework versions of the host server.

### Details
In `lib/setup-sandbox.js:436-466`, the `CallSite` class overrides `getThis()` and `getFunction()` with `undefined` to prevent host

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows sandboxed code to disclose sensitive host file paths, potentially exposing directory structures, library paths, and framework versions. This could aid attackers in reconnaissance and increase the likelihood of further exploitation.
recommended_actions: Update vm2 to the latest patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict access to the sandbox environment and monitor for unusual activity or file path disclosures.
Implement additional logging and intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: vm2 Host Promise Resolution Preserves Object Identity Across Sandbox Boundary
description:
### Summary

A sandbox boundary violation in **vm2** allows host object identity to cross into the sandbox through host Promise resolution.

When a host-side Promise that resolves to a host object is exposed to the sandbox, the value delivered to the sandbox `.then()` callback preserves host identity. This allows the sandbox to interact with the host object directly, including:

- Performing identity checks using host-side `WeakMap`
- Mutating host object state from inside the sandbox

This beha

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44000.

title: CVE-2026-42511 - The BOOTP file field is written to the lease file without escaping embedded double-quotes, allowing
description:
The BOOTP file field is written to the lease file without escaping embedded double-quotes, allowing injection of arbitrary dhclient.conf directives.  When the lease file is subsequently re-parsed by dhclient, e.g., after a system restart, an attacker-controlled field from the lease is passed to dhclient-script(8), which evaluates it.

A rogue DHCP server may be able to execute arbirary code as root on a system running dhclient.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a rogue DHCP server to inject arbitrary dhclient.conf directives, potentially leading to remote code execution as root. This could compromise system integrity, data confidentiality, and availability.
recommended_actions: Apply patches or updates provided by the vendor to address CVE-2026-42511 immediately.
Configure network environments to prevent unauthorized DHCP servers from being introduced, such as using DHCP snooping or static IP configurations.
Monitor and audit DHCP-related logs and configurations to detect any signs of exploitation or suspicious activity.

description: Open redirect vulnerability exists in Multiple laser printers and MFPs which implement Ricoh Web Image Monitor. When accessing a specially crafted URL, the user may be redirected to an arbitrary website. As a result, the user may become a victim of a phishing attack.

title: CVE-2026-7446 - A vulnerability was detected in VetCoders mcp-server-semgrep 1.0.0. This affects the function analyz
description: A vulnerability was detected in VetCoders mcp-server-semgrep 1.0.0. This affects the function analyze_results/filter_results/export_results/compare_results/scan_directory/create_rule of the file src/index.ts of the component MCP Interface. The manipulation of the argument ID results in os command injection. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. Upgrading to version 1.0.1 is able to mitigate this issue. The patch is identified as 141335da044e53c3f5b315e0386e01238405b771. It is advisable to upgrade the affected component.

description: A security vulnerability has been detected in ZachHandley ZMCPTools up to 0.2.2. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file src/managers/ResourceManager.ts of the component MCP Log Resource Handler. The manipulation of the argument dirname leads to path traversal. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.
